payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: That would have been about ten years ago.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Now available in the merch store!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'm not going to be able to run the game tonight. I can't focus. Take two.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Neither can the fans, steamy Dr. Mercer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I will never live that down. It's great when you're a new actor in LA and everyone says, "Yeah, you got to get some head shots, and I know all about it. You got to get those costume and character shots in."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is that what it was? Yes! Oh, I love it!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: When you show up, right off the truck, you don't know what you're doing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you need some archetypes. Come with a doctor outfit, come with a regular business suit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: To be fair, I got a few gigs that way, by doing a few character shots.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I've done a lot of doctors. No, all that photo has done is this. That is literally the most it's ever done.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You're like 17 and a half here, right?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: That is where we left off. In this case, guys, we have roughly two weeks of downtime. I'm going to go around and ask what you would like to accomplish, and if moments require rolls, moments require roleplay, anything like that, we'll jump into it. I would like to say, who wants to go first?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I've done a lot of doctors. No, all that photo has done is this. That is literally the most it's ever done.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.text: That would have been about ten years ago.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So early 30s?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes. We'll go with that one. Anyway, back to the story at hand! Upon completing your journey, checking in with The Gentleman, you were paid the ripe sum that you had discussed. After taking your newfound friend from Shadycreek Run, Ophelia, back to The Evening Nip, you exited with your new gold and platinum in hand. After a celebratory drink to the memory of Molly, you grabbed Caduceus and led him into the city of Zadash-- the largest city you've ever seen by leaps and bounds-- to see what kind of ways you can corrupt this new, fresh mind, free out of the Savalierwood.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's so exciting!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: This next expense will be 50 gold pieces for the set of tinkerer's tools.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep, I have that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, step two! I'm going to take my gun.

preceding_turns.text: Your what?

preceding_turns.text: Oh, I stole a gun. You weren't there for that?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I stole a gun!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You got a gat?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I stole a gun from a guard. I've got one bullet.

preceding_turns.text: Yeah, like one round, single shot?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! Don't fuck with me, man! I will blow you away, motherfucker!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit! Okay!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Unless I miss, and then it's nothing. No, I would like to take apart the gun. I'd like to take apart the gun and figure out how the hammer works, how the gunpowder ignites. Is it a pack of powder like a bullet, or is it loose powder with a ball?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, first off, do you have or own tinkerer's tools?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Have what?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Tinkerer's tools. I'll go and buy them!

payload.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.text: One explosive arrow.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Welcome to tinkering, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.text: Okay. Then I have one explosive arrow, maybe. Great. I'd also like to purchase a pair of gloves or have them made.

payload.trigger_phrase: you make your way to the Pentamarket
payload.raw_text: Okay. Midday, you make your way to the Pentamarket, to the very vibrant center area of the marketplace there. There's a number of intersecting pathways that help divide between all the various carts and tents that open up. At this point, I'd say it's maybe late morning or so. A lot of the curled up curtains in the front are being affixed to the top or have been for a while. The smells of fresh baked goods, cooking stews, and various other treats and snacks that are being presented as part of the shopping experience catch your nostrils as you move through. You hear music playing in the distance. For all the chaos that life has thrown you in the past 30 days, it feels nice to step into a place that's so filled with life and lack of understanding of how dark the world is at the fringes of this empire, both with the war going on and some of the things you've encountered. It's nice to step into this space and you catch yourself instinctually smiling and finding it infectious to brighten your mood. You turn to see the darkened green wood with gold accoutrements that marks the exterior of The Invulnerable Vagrant. The front door is closed, but appears to be not locked for any particular reason.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay, all right. Well, what are you guys looking for?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Go see that beautiful baby, Travis.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: I want to go to Pumat.

preceding_turns.text: Oh, well we have some fellows you could meet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

preceding_turns.text: That's great.

preceding_turns.text: I have a lot of money.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm coming.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go shopping.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So the four of you, Jester, Caduceus, Caleb, and Beauregard, are going shopping.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, all right. Well, what are you guys looking for?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS

preceding_turns.text: Go see that beautiful baby, Travis.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Going on a trip.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I want to go to Pumat.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: But the aesthetic is there, yeah. As you step forward, the door opens up and leads you inside, and there the same warmly lit interior with the glowing chandelier above greets you. Across the way, sitting behind the desk, face down in a book, one of the various Pumats, who looks up and, "Oh, why hello there! Yeah, oh, it's been a while. Sorry, took me a second to recognize you. You're very silhouetted by the bright sunshine outside you right now and it's a bit dark interior, so come on up!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I have such a clear picture in my head, now that we've built it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Now that we've physically built it, yeah. It's a little larger in-world than the one that we did.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But the aesthetic.

payload.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.text: He seems like a decent boss. That seems nice.

preceding_turns.text: "Not too bad. People say you want to be your own boss, well--" (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's really good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It feels weird to interrupt whatever this is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm hungry, so.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Unfortunately, we don't really sell much in the realm of food items."

preceding_turns.text: I am in the market for some things of arcane nature. Oh, I see you're reading a book. What are you reading?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

preceding_turns.text: "No."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So, you know.

preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Reaches in, pulls out a big batch of garlic. Just a big old batch of garlic in a small netted sack. She goes, "Garlic. Good for cooking and also for keeping away... vampyr!" (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You know what we could do? Get the garlic, sprinkle the Dust of Deliciousness on the garlic, and feed it to the vampyr!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Then they'd be in pain and very susceptible to--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a religion check, Jester.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Would I know this stuff? A natural one!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This plan sounds perfectly sound. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I want to see what else is in the sack before-- We shouldn't negotiate until we know everything.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, this is true.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, for the sake of brevity--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: For the sake of brevity.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a whole slew of strange knickknacks--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And we sit there while she pulls out the entire bag.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No. For this, she's like, "This will run 250 gold pieces. But it will keep you from dying in places where you will probably most likely be dying."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll take the garlic.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well. Two gold." Which is really expensive for fucking garlic.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm thinking, that's-- One gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: One gold?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Hardcore negotiations.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It is yours."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) Secrets!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's still overpriced for garlic.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's so overpriced for garlic.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How much is the healing potion?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's good garlic. Never can pay too-- Yeah, it's really nice garlic.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Heirloom. Artisinal.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is actually pretty artisanal garlic.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Artisanal garlic.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It came out of the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so yeah. This would be a greater healing potion.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ooh!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's expensive.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Maybe not from her.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Ask.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She just charged two gold for garlic.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: That would be Nicodranas. Nicodranas from Zadash, it's a little over a week to get there, I'd say.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Sure. Yeah. The previous events have rocked Fjord a little bit. Are there any bars or taverns in Zadash that may house frequent sailors or people of trade, merchants?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sailors, not so much. The Empire is landlocked aside from the very far north, and those waters are in the frigid depths. It's a lot of ice floes and treacherous water up there. So there is very little passage up north. The Menagerie Coast, as you know, is where all of the port towns are, and that's the relationship between the Empire and The Menagerie Coast. That agreement is based on the fact that, essentially, the Menagerie Coast holds all the long-term imports and exports in the palm of their hand.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I know that I've made that trip. How far is it from Zadash back to the northernmost part of the Menagerie Coast?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You make it as far south as Trostenwald before the frustration wells up inside you. It's been a series of dead ends. You've asked around and listened, and anyone who would've crossed paths with some of the shipping lanes that you're familiar with and that Sabian was familiar with, none of them are passing this far north. You assume that to continue this, you would have to go to the Menagerie Coast, and that would involve a thorough passage through Nicodranas and the various wharf taverns and seeing what old connections you might be able to dredge up in that time, but that's going to take you a little while.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That would be Nicodranas. Nicodranas from Zadash, it's a little over a week to get there, I'd say.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (sighs) I think Fjord's going to take a steed and start heading south by himself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What? He's going on walkabout?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's going on a walkabout.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: While he heads south, I'd like to adopt various disguises as I stop in inns, taverns, places of trade, and carefully hear if anyone is mentioning the name "Sabian," if people are talking about ships, and try and casually make my way further south, knowing that I have an amount of time to get back north. I'd like to try to make my way down and see what I can hear and find out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Make three successive investigation checks for me, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay. 12, nine, and a six.

payload.raw_text: Okay. We'll get to that. First, you guys make your way to the archive and you enter there, and the following morning Caleb is very tired-looking, but also hard to tell.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: All righty. Cat Frumpkin has returned.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: There is only one Frumpkin. I stayed up all night doing all of those things because they're very time-intensive and would have went to find Beauregard, very tired and ready to go, to stay at the library as long as I am able. She said a number of days, and when those days are up, the thing that I would do is spend as much time that is left with the-- I would borrow Jester's haversack, if she would let me, and spend time with the dodecahedron.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. We'll get to that. First, you guys make your way to the archive and you enter there, and the following morning Caleb is very tired-looking, but also hard to tell.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. Cat Frumpkin has returned.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: There is only one Frumpkin. I stayed up all night doing all of those things because they're very time-intensive and would have went to find Beauregard, very tired and ready to go, to stay at the library as long as I am able. She said a number of days, and when those days are up, the thing that I would do is spend as much time that is left with the-- I would borrow Jester's haversack, if she would let me, and spend time with the dodecahedron.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Well, she's been sent to assess political threats beyond the eastern border. Been gone for about 30 days and is said to return in the next 45 before we send anyone after her. But I'm sure she's fine."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Making your way into the archive, it's fairly bustling in comparison to what you know. It's either dead or there's a handful of people going through it. Today it's a handful of people going through. You're like, "Oh wow, there's four people in here researching right now."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No one ever cares about books this much. It's weird.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Within a short time-- what are you looking to do, Beauregard?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I ask around and see if Dairon is there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: One of the monks goes, "Let me go inquire. I'll be right back," and wanders up the stairs. You see them descend the stairs with another figure. Gray robes with blue lining, hood pulled back, and there you see Zeenoth.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He steps down and goes, "Beauregard. I wasn't aware you had returned."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hi.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Hi. Dairon, unfortunately, is still out on the front lines."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. Tubo brings you to a chamber you haven't been in in a while, which is the formal training grounds beneath the archive. There is a set of double doors in the far end that blend in mostly with the dark wood that a lot of the bookcases are crafted out of. It leads you down a set of stairs into what is essentially a training pit with very heavily packed sand and dirt, and there are a number of wooden poles that emerge from beneath that have all sorts of skewed extensions. This is where a lot of forms are practiced and a lot of defensive maneuvers are utilized. You were brought through in this area for training in waves time and again, but it's been a while since you've been down here, since you've been abroad, and there's no other people there training currently. Tubo brings you down. "Very well. Show me what you've got." For the sake of brevity--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is he an expositor?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hmm. Yeah, all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right. I shall return. Good seeing you again."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Zeenoth's patient smile fades as he turns. Within a short time, you see coming from down the same staircase, Tubo, his arms folded in front of him as he descends and turns toward you. "So you are Beauregard, correct?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "And you're looking for training while your expositor is away, correct?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I mean--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Follow me."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Also, I got this guy with me. Can he get a room and a library pass?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Zeenoth, who's a little bit behind, goes, "I'll accompany him in his reading, if that's all right?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Zeenoth, Caleb. Caleb, Zeenoth.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We've met, in Trostenwald."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hallo.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Hello. Please, lead the way to where you'd wish to read."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, thank you. Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Mm-hmm.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yes. Roll up a new character. Tubo splatters you across the wall. You spar for a bit, showing him the limits of your capabilities, and he's deftly, with a smile on his face, dodging, parrying. You get a few good hits in and he backs away. "Very good," and then begins to overwhelm you with his assault, but never enough to inflict pain, just enough to throw you off your guard until a moment he turns around and puts his hands up into a cross once more and goes, "Four days. I need four days with you."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You die.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "That you are an apt pupil. Now, our time is done. I have things to do. And four days with you has been more than enough."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And one more.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Spend another ki point. That's two ki points now. You get to roll to reduce the third one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that one does no damage. You catch one arrow out of the air. It just barely nicks your shoulder. The other one you catch just as it scrapes past the side of your arm. The third one you catch in the middle of the air with no impact. In the blink of an eye, you've deflected all three arrows shot at the same time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I turn to Tubo and I go  Did you see that?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I did. And with that, your training is complete."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. I don't get to kill those guys?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No!" He hands the shortbow to the other one and they all head back upstairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, I'm sure you're really lovely people.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Impressive. Dairon was not wrong about you."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, what has-- Dairon's been talking about me? What has she been saying?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: There's nothing about beacons, nothing about reincarnation. However, it does speak of the dynasty worshiping something other, something that is new and dangerous as far as the Cobalt Soul is concerned, with what little research they have. They refer to it as "The Luxon" and seek to bring all under its burning banner. They've rallied to the surface of Ghor Dranas, casting the skies in eternal midnight that spreads with their dominion. They've begun taking the ravenous hordes of beast-men and terrors of the Xhorhasian wastes, and indoctrinate them under this new false god. You also learn that a number of people, mostly children of adolescent age, have been found abducted in the night by the Kryn on occasion, though the number has steeply grown this past generation, years after the first open conflicts with the Kryn began. The last thing you discover is the leader of the dynasty is known as Empress Leylas Kryn. She is called an "umavi" by them, though the word's meaning is unknown by current research. Accounts of the Empress speak of her as a powerful and dangerous warrior on the battlefield. And well over 600 years old or more, as records date back that far since she arrived in local history. That's what you've discovered about the Kryn Dynasty and Xhorhas.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You see? You know it, too. That means you looked at me because you knew.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Of course. As soon as I read the word I was like-- Without fail.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pray to the great god titty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We missed you. But for those who were able to move past the boundaries to research more of what little is known of the Kryn Dynasty, there is little in iconography under Lolth or Tharizdun. And what remains of that is ancient and crumbling and largely ignored by the society.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do I find any mention of these beacons-- he mentioned beacons, plural-- and reincarnation or multiple lives?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The things you discover, from what's available to you in the archive: time is a constant, the motion moving forward unyielding. Some powerful arcane influences can accelerate or slow the rate at which time passes locally, even stop it. There are some stories from the Age of Arcanum that speak of the greatest of sages experimenting with reversing the forward momentum of time, and supposedly with some success. But the process apparently was so taxing and the energies required so extensive that it nearly killed those who passed through this natural flow. That's as much as you can pick up on the specific research involving that shift of time. They're more footnotes and like a, "We don't mess with that because those who tried have mostly been disintegrated or destroyed in the process."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I spend a day reading about that. I will spend every remaining day reading about-- I was rushed the first time I came here, so I'm going to spend every day left reading about the weave of time and any mortals' attempts to control it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The weave of time.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's some restricted reading bullshit.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yes, but they existed close to 1500 years ago.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Nearly killed. Does it talk about any of the people who were nearly killed but weren't?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No, it's near the crossguard, in the center of the hilt. When you look at it, it doesn't seem to move or shift or pay any attention. Practicing the next few days and trying some things out, you focus some of your arcane gifts towards an object, casting Witch Bolt towards a nearby rock or low, dried, and forgotten trunk of a tree. At the moment the incantation begins, the eye shifts to look towards the target of the spell. The spell explodes and you look back at the hilt, and it's already reverted back to where it was before.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I was like, I feel like I came up-- Oh, there's more shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I was like, are you sure that's all you want to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. We can just say on the way back from those visits, at night when I made camp, I want to summon the falchion and spend more time with it. Examine it, specifically the eye, if it has that sort of-- in the letter, it looked like there was a gash into the sphere itself. Does it represent that, is it smooth? Does it move?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The eye, where it sits in the hilt-- and it only sits on one side, it's not both sides that contain the eye, because there aren't two pupils. There is a singular eye that is embedded in it, that is looking out what would essentially be towards the left.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it near the crossguard, or is it near the pommel?

payload.trigger_phrase: you make your way to The Evening
payload.raw_text: Sure, all right. So you make your way to The Evening Nip. By the time you've returned, it's been the better part of the two-week period of time, and you guys are also generally worried the fact that Fjord just vanished during this time period.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Is it near the crossguard, or is it near the pommel?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: No, it's near the crossguard, in the center of the hilt. When you look at it, it doesn't seem to move or shift or pay any attention. Practicing the next few days and trying some things out, you focus some of your arcane gifts towards an object, casting Witch Bolt towards a nearby rock or low, dried, and forgotten trunk of a tree. At the moment the incantation begins, the eye shifts to look towards the target of the spell. The spell explodes and you look back at the hilt, and it's already reverted back to where it was before.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: But it did seem to react to when the spell was cast.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Creepy. What the hell does that mean? Okay. When I get back, could I make my way to The Gentleman for just a quick convo?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Sure, all right. So you make your way to The Evening Nip. By the time you've returned, it's been the better part of the two-week period of time, and you guys are also generally worried the fact that Fjord just vanished during this time period.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it near the crossguard, or is it near the pommel?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it's near the crossguard, in the center of the hilt. When you look at it, it doesn't seem to move or shift or pay any attention. Practicing the next few days and trying some things out, you focus some of your arcane gifts towards an object, casting Witch Bolt towards a nearby rock or low, dried, and forgotten trunk of a tree. At the moment the incantation begins, the eye shifts to look towards the target of the spell. The spell explodes and you look back at the hilt, and it's already reverted back to where it was before.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Super fast.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But it did seem to react to when the spell was cast.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Creepy. What the hell does that mean? Okay. When I get back, could I make my way to The Gentleman for just a quick convo?

payload.trigger_phrase: You make your way to the subterranean
payload.raw_text: You make your way to the subterranean basement of The Evening Nip. The Gentleman is not currently present, but upon asking around, and you wait for about 20 minutes or so, he does return from the storage room to the side where you had previously been led down and through the Underworks. As he comes in, he sees you and sits on the other side of the table, throwing his leg over one side and sitting at a very comfortable and casual way across from you. "Fjord, I'm being told that you're looking for me."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: You guys are going to shop, ah!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You make your way to the subterranean basement of The Evening Nip. The Gentleman is not currently present, but upon asking around, and you wait for about 20 minutes or so, he does return from the storage room to the side where you had previously been led down and through the Underworks. As he comes in, he sees you and sits on the other side of the table, throwing his leg over one side and sitting at a very comfortable and casual way across from you. "Fjord, I'm being told that you're looking for me."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Super fast.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But it did seem to react to when the spell was cast.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Creepy. What the hell does that mean? Okay. When I get back, could I make my way to The Gentleman for just a quick convo?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure, all right. So you make your way to The Evening Nip. By the time you've returned, it's been the better part of the two-week period of time, and you guys are also generally worried the fact that Fjord just vanished during this time period.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He left while we were shopping.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Big surprise.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Travis is shouting.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You guys are going to shop, ah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (poof sound)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What was it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Spooky exit.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "The jewel of the coast. I've been a number of times, though it's been years since I've been able to leave my work here alone. So if you're looking to expand your portfolio beyond Zadash, that is commendable. The one contact I could recommend in Port Damali would be Father Dwondaff Pierce."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I have some reaches in places, yes. That's quite a scope you're requesting. I would say it's a good bet that most criminal elements you would encounter there are not at my network, except for a handful. It might be more useful to you to know the ones to trust."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's where I was going with that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Okay. Port Damali, you say?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's where I'm from.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Really?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Indeed.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "That is all right. Thank you for your honesty. It is hard for him to be gone for so long, then come back, and so swiftly go again. Maybe I had put too much faith."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (stuttering) Hm?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I have had a number of uncomfortable dreams in the week, and... I cannot sense Lucien."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Tell me about these dreams.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh god. Oh, that's good. 18.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "They are cold and sad. I just see him alone, and I've tried to focus. He is not nearby. I have a very hollow feeling about this."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) God, why was I gone for six weeks? Okay. (normal volume) Yes, unfortunately your dreams may have some merit. I myself was missing for most of our journey, and it seems, unfortunately, that Lucien befell a terrible fate.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That was what I was fearing."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry to be the one to let you know. If I was there I could give you more detail, but I found out much in the same way you are.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It is all right. I have started a new life here, and it has been serving me well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We of the Task Takers, we were undergoing a ritual that he had orchestrated with an ally of his, a woman of magical renown. He said that this was something that was meant to lead us to this city he promised, a place where we could learn of a great many powers and secrets. We believed him. He was very charismatic in his cruelty at times. But he did not survive."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And then you saw him here for the first time in quite a while, yes?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Two years.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Two years? Two years. Are you-- and forgive me if this is too personal-- are you expecting to see him again?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I wasn't expecting to see him this last time. I feel like whatever grace brought him back to us, maybe it was your carelessness that took him from us again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Indeed, maybe it was. You have my apologies and my condolences.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He's there most of the day, and in the evening he appears to go nap somewhere within the Platinum House.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (snoring) (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But yeah, the midday stakeout appears to go well. What do you wish to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How often does he come back?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You guys approach in the evening, together? Are you disguising yourself as well?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Your Anarchist's Cookbook.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: My Anarchist's Cookbook, a couple brochures for the Traveler, and I steal Cad's straw hat that he bought-- sorry-- and, yeah, we're going to make the Platinum Dragon look like a really cool farmer.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Okay. So we're going to mess with the statue.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's what I do. Is it stupid or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What if someone comes in? We kill everyone in the place?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, if somebody comes in, you need to distract them, okay? Don't let anybody see me, because this might take a while.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So I'm the distraction and the lookout.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're the distraction. I also Invoke Duplicity to where it looks like I'm still there on the outside and I can also be hanging out with you so you don't look like a random person.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What do you disguise yourself to be?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The old woman that was in the other, yeah.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. Jester, now taking the elderly form of a silver-scaled, very well-respected, Herald of the House Kumas. You approach the temple a few hours after sundown, to where the interest in worship tends to fade as folks go back to home. It's mostly just a few caretakers that watch it through the night and some lost souls that have found their way there in the evening and they need guidance.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Okay. So we're going to mess with the statue.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's what I do. Is it stupid or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What if someone comes in? We kill everyone in the place?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, if somebody comes in, you need to distract them, okay? Don't let anybody see me, because this might take a while.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're the distraction. I also Invoke Duplicity to where it looks like I'm still there on the outside and I can also be hanging out with you so you don't look like a random person.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What do you disguise yourself to be?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The old woman that was in the other, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys approach in the evening, together? Are you disguising yourself as well?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, no. You know what I should disguise myself as? The sermon guy. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So I'm the distraction and the lookout.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're the distraction. I also Invoke Duplicity to where it looks like I'm still there on the outside and I can also be hanging out with you so you don't look like a random person.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What do you disguise yourself to be?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The old woman that was in the other, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys approach in the evening, together? Are you disguising yourself as well?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, no. You know what I should disguise myself as? The sermon guy. Yeah.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It works out okay. Having all spent the majority of these two weeks handling your business, wandering through Zadash and beyond, returning, what do you wish to do? You guys have regathered in the Leaky Tap.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome back. Before we get back into this, we have our winner of the giveaway, of the fantastic Deven Rue-carved cherry wood dice tower system. The winner is Mellydew. Congratulations. We'll get your info and get this sent out to you ASAP, so thank you for being a part. Laura will be joining us shortly; she's finishing feeding the baby.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, feed the baby, give it life.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Feed me!
